member.name: C:__namemax
member.summary: The maximum length of symbol names, excluding any NULLs.

member.name: C:undefined
member.summary: An invalid value (-1) as a boolean for some natives (most notably the vehicle param ones) that can skip `true`/`false` parameters.

member.name: F:__OPEN_MP_VERSION
member.remarks: If running on SA:MP this will remain 0, open.mp will set it. open.mp versions look like: 01.04.02.0544 Thus they are encoded here as: (major << 28) | (minor << 21) | (patch << 14) | (prerel)

member.name: F:__SAMP_INCLUDES_VERSION
member.remarks:
Version examples:
0.3.DL R1 - 03D010
0.3.7 R3 - 037030
0.3.7 R2-2 - 037022
0.3.7 R1-2 - 037012
0.3.7 - 037000
0.3z R4 - 030700
0.3z R3 - 030700
0.3z R2-1 - 030700
0.3z R1-2 - 030700
0.3z - 030700
0.3x R2 patch 1 - 030621
0.3x R2 - 030620
0.3x R1-2 - 030612
0.3x - 030600
0.3e - 030500
0.3d - 030400
0.3c - 030300
0.3b - 030200
0.3a - 030100
0.2X - 02A000
0.2.2 R3 - 022300 Rough rules:
Uses (roughtly) BCD. Special versions are denoted outside 0-9. 0.1.2c R4-5 | | || | | 0 1 23 4 5 = 0x012345 (assuming c is the third revision)
0.2X becomes 02A000 because it is basically 0.2.3, but not, while higher than 0.2.2 so can't be 020400 (for example). Also, its a capital letter, so doesn't use the revision method.
P.S. Making a consistent scheme for SA:MP versions is REALLY hard.
open.mp releases can use `A` as the first digit.

member.name: M:@timer
member.summary: A timer event occurred.
member.remarks:
This function executes after the configured delay or interval expires. See settimer to set the delay or interval. Depending on the timing precision of the host, the call may occur later than the delay that was set.
If the timer was set as a "single-shot", it must be ex plicitly set again for a next execution for the @timerfunction. If the timer is set to be repetitive, @timer will continue to be called with the set interval until it is disabled with another call to settimer.
member.returns: The return value of this function is currently ignored.

member.name: M:AddRejectedHit
member.syntax: AddRejectedHit(playerid, damagedid, reason, weapon, i1, i2, i3)

member.name: M:AddStaticVehicle
member.syntax: AddStaticVehicle(modelid, spawnX, spawnY, spawnZ, angle, colour1, colour2)
member.param: The Model ID for the vehicle
Float The x coordinate of the spawnpoint of this vehicle
Float The y coordinate of the spawnpoint of this vehicle
Float The z coordinate of the spawnpoint of this vehicle
Float Direction of vehicle - angle
The primary colour ID. -1 for random (random colour chosen by client)
The secondary colour ID. -1 for random (random colour chosen by client)
member.summary: Adds a 'static' vehicle (models are pre-loaded for players) to the gamemode.

ul.li: The vehicle ID of the vehicle created (between 1 and MAX_VEHICLES).
INVALID_VEHICLE_ID (65535) if vehicle was not created (vehicle limit reached or invalid vehicle model ID passed).

member.remarks: Can only be used when the server first starts (under OnGameModeInit).

member.name: M:AddStaticVehicleEx
member.syntax: AddStaticVehicleEx(modelid, spawnX, spawnY, spawnZ, angle, colour1, colour2, respawnDelay, addSiren)
member.param: The Model ID for the vehicle
Float The x coordinate of the spawnpoint of this vehicle
Float The y coordinate of the spawnpoint of this vehicle
Float The z coordinate of the spawnpoint of this vehicle
Float Direction of vehicle - angle
The primary colour ID. -1 for random (random colour chosen by client)
The secondary colour ID. -1 for random (random colour chosen by client)
The delay until the car is respawned without a driver, in seconds
bool Added in 0.3.7; will not work in earlier versions. Enables the vehicle to have a siren, providing the vehicle has a horn (optional=0)
member.summary: Adds a 'static' vehicle (models are pre-loaded for players)to the gamemode. Differs from AddStaticVehicle in only one way: allows a respawn time to be set for when the vehicle is left unoccupied by the driver.

ul.li: The vehicle ID of the vehicle created (between 1 and MAX_VEHICLES).
INVALID_VEHICLE_ID (65535) if vehicle was not created (vehicle limit reached or invalid vehicle model ID passed).

member.remarks: Can only be used when the server first starts (under OnGameModeInit).

member.name: M:ApplyAnimation
member.syntax: ApplyAnimation(playerid, animationLibrary[], animationName[], delta, loop, lockX, lockY, freeze, time, forceSync)
member.param: The ID of the player to apply the animation to
[] The animation library from which to apply an animation
[] The name of the animation to apply, within the specified library
Float The speed to play the animation (use 4.1)
bool If set to 1, the animation will loop. If set to 0, the animation will play once
bool If set to 0, the player is returned to their old x coordinate once the animation is complete (for animations that move the player such as walking). 1 will not return them to their old position
bool Same as above but for the y axis. Should be kept the same as the previous parameter
bool Setting this to 1 will freeze the player at the end of the animation. 0 will not
Timer in milliseconds. For a never-ending loop it should be 0

t_FORCE_SYNC Set to 1 to make server sync the animation with all other players in streaming radius. 2 works same as 1, but will ONLY apply the animation to streamed-in players, but NOT the actual player being animated (useful for npc animations and persistent animations when players are being streamed) (optional=0)
member.summary: Apply an animation to a player.
member.remarks: The optional parameter, which defaults to 0, in most cases is not needed since players sync animations themselves. The parameter can force all players who can see to play the animation regardless of whether the player is performing that animation. This is useful in circumstances where the player can't sync the animation themselves. For example, they may be paused.
An invalid animation library will crash the player's game.
member.returns: This function always returns 1, even if the player specified does not exist, or any of the parameters are invalid (e.g. invalid library).

member.name: M:CallRemoteFunction
member.param: [] Public function's name
[] Tag/format of each variable
{LANDING_GEAR_STATE,Bit,Bitmap,XML,XMLEntry,Group,INI,Language,Style,DB,DBResult,Menu,Text,PlayerText,Text3D,PlayerText3D,File,Float,CUSTOM_TAG_TYPES,_} 'Indefinite' number of arguments of any tag
member.summary: Calls a public function in any script that is loaded.
member.returns: The value that the last public function returned.
member.remarks: CallRemoteFunction crashes the server if it's passing an empty string.

Specifier string placeholders:
c - a single character
d - an integer (whole) number
i - an integer (whole) number
x - a number in hexadecimal notation
f - a floating point number
s - a string

member.name: M:ClearAnimations
member.param: The ID of the player to clear the animations of
t_FORCE_SYNC Set to 1 to force playerid to sync the animation with other players in streaming radius (optional=0)
member.summary: Clears all animations for the given player (it also cancels all current tasks such as jetpacking,parachuting,entering vehicles, driving (removes player out of vehicle), swimming, etc.. ).
member.remarks: ClearAnimations doesn't do anything when the animation ends if we pass 1 for the freeze parameter in ApplyAnimation.

Unlike some other ways to remove player from a vehicle, this will also reset the vehicle's velocity to zero, instantly stopping the car. Player will appear on top of the vehicle with the same location as he was in his car seat.
member.returns: This function always returns 1, even when the player specified is not connected.

member.name: M:CreateActor
member.param: The model ID (skin ID) the actor should have
Float The x coordinate to create the actor at
Float The y coordinate to create the actor at
Float The z coordinate to create the actor at
Float The facing angle (rotation) for the actor to have
member.summary: Create a static 'actor' in the world. These 'actors' are like NPCs, however they have limited functionality. They do not take up server player slots.
member.remarks:
Actors are completely separate from NPCs. They do NOT use player IDs/slots on the server and CANNOT be handled like NPCs.
Actors are limited to 1000 (MAX_ACTORS).
Actors can be pushed by vehicles, use a timer to put them back at their positions.
member.returns:
The created Actor ID (start at 0).
INVALID_ACTOR_ID (65535) If the actor limit (1000) is reached.

member.name: M:CreatePlayerTextDraw
member.param: The ID of the player to create the textdraw for
Float x-Coordinate
Float y-Coordinate
[] The text in the textdraw. May be optionally formatted.
member.summary: Creates a textdraw for a single player. This can be used as a way around the global text-draw limit.
member.remarks:
If you choose values for y that are less than 1, the first text row will be invisible and only the shadow is visible.
If part of the text is off-screen, the colour of the text will not show, only the shadow (if enabled) will.

Maximum length of textdraw is 800 characters.
If you use colour codes (such as ~R~ ~G~) beyond 255th character the legacy (SA:MP) client will crash trying to display the textdraw.
Keyboard key mapping codes (such as ~k~~VEHICLE_ENTER_EXIT~ Doesn't work beyond 255th character.

The x, y coordinate is the top left coordinate for the text draw area based on a 640x480 "canvas" (irrespective of screen resolution). If you plan on using PlayerTextDrawAlignment with alignment 3 (right), the x, y coordinate is the top right coordinate for the text draw.
This function merely CREATES the textdraw, you must use PlayerTextDrawShow to show it to a player.
It is recommended to use WHOLE numbers instead of decimal positions when creating player textdraws to ensure resolution friendly design.
Player-textdraws are automatically destroyed when a player disconnects.
member.returns: The ID of the created textdraw.

member.name: M:CreateVehicle
member.syntax: CreateVehicle(modelid, spawnX, spawnY, spawnZ, angle, colour1, colour2, respawnDelay, addSiren)
member.param: The model for the vehicle
Float The x coordinate for the vehicle
Float The y coordinate for the vehicle
Float The z coordinate for the vehicle
Float The facing angle for the vehicle
The primary colour ID
The secondary colour ID
The delay until the car is respawned without a driver in seconds. Using -1 will prevent the vehicle from respawning
bool Added in 0.3.7; will not work in earlier versions. Enables the vehicle to have a siren, providing the vehicle has a horn (optional=false)
member.summary: Creates a vehicle in the world. Can be used in place of AddStaticVehicleEx at any time in the script.
member.remarks: Trains can only be added with AddStaticVehicle and AddStaticVehicleEx.
member.returns:
The vehicle ID of the vehicle created (1 to MAX_VEHICLES).
INVALID_VEHICLE_ID (65535) if vehicle was not created (vehicle limit reached or invalid vehicle model ID passed).

member.name: M:DamagePlayer
member.syntax: DamagePlayer(playerid, amount, issuerid, weaponid, bodypart, ignore_armour)

member.name: M:DestroyActor
member.param: The ID of the actor to destroy. Returned by CreateActor
member.summary: Destroy an actor which was created with CreateActor.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The actor with the ID specified does not exist.

member.name: M:DestroyVehicle
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to destroy
member.summary: Destroy a vehicle. It will disappear instantly.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The vehicle does not exist.

member.name: M:GetActorPoolSize
member.summary: Gets the highest actorid created on the server.
member.returns: The highest actorid created on the server or 0 if there are no created actors.

member.name: M:GetConsoleVarAsInt
member.param: [] The name of the integer variable to get the value of
member.summary: Get the integer value of a console variable.
member.remarks: Type varlist in the server console to display a list of available console variables and their types.
member.returns: The value of the specified console variable. 0 if the specified console variable is not an integer or doesn't exist.

member.name: M:GetPlayerAnimationIndex
member.param: ID of the player of whom you want to get the animation index of
member.summary: Returns the index of any running applied animations.
member.returns: 0 if there is no animation applied.

member.name: M:GetPlayerArmour
member.param: The ID of the player that you want to get the armour of
Float & The float to to store the armour in, passed by reference
member.summary: This function stores the armour of a player into a variable.
member.remarks: Even though the armour can be set to near infinite values on the server side, the individual clients will only report values up to 255. Anything higher will wrap around; 256 becomes 0, 257 becomes 1, etc.
Armour is obtained rounded to integers: set 50.15, but get 50.0
The function's name is armour, not armor (Americanized).
member.returns:
1 - success.
0 - failure (i.e. player not connected).

member.name: M:GetPlayerDistanceFromPoint
member.param: The ID of the player to calculate the distance from
Float The x map coordinate
Float The y map coordinate
Float The z map coordinate
member.summary: Calculate the distance between a player and a map coordinate.
member.returns: The distance between the player and the point as a float.

member.name: M:GetPlayerFacingAngle
member.param: The player you want to get the angle of
Float & The Float to store the angle in, passed by reference
member.summary: Gets the angle a player is facing.
member.remarks: Angles are reversed in GTA:SA; 90 degrees would be East in the real world, but in GTA:SA 90 degrees is in fact West. North and South are still 0/360 and 180. To convert this, simply do 360 - angle.
Angles returned when inside a vehicle is rarely correct. To get the correct facing angle while inside a vehicle, use GetVehicleZAngle.

member.name: M:GetPlayerFightingStyle
member.param: The ID of the player to get the fighting style of
member.summary: Get the fighting style the player currently using.
member.returns: The ID of the fighting style of the player.

member.name: M:GetPlayerHealth
member.param: The ID of the player
Float & Float to store health, passed by reference
member.summary: The function GetPlayerHealth allows you to retrieve the health of a player. Useful for cheat detection, among other things.
member.remarks:
Even though the health can be set to near infinite values on the server side, the individual clients will only report values up to 255. Anything higher will wrap around; 256 becomes 0, 257 becomes 1, etc.
Health is obtained rounded to integers: set 50.15, but get 50.0
member.returns:
1 - success.
0 - failure (i.e. player not connected).

member.name: M:GetPlayerLastShotVectors
member.param: The ID of the player to get the last bullet shot information of
Float & A float variable in which to save the x coordinate of where the bullet originated from
Float & A float variable in which to save the y coordinate of where the bullet originated from
Float & A float variable in which to save the z coordinate of where the bullet originated from
Float & A float variable in which to save the x coordinate of where the bullet hit
Float & A float variable in which to save the y coordinate of where the bullet hit
Float & A float variable in which to save the z coordinate of where the bullet hit
member.summary: Retrieves the start and end (hit) position of the last bullet a player fired.
member.remarks: This function will only work when lag compensation is enabled.
If the player hit nothing, the hit positions will be 0. This means you can't currently calculate how far a bullet travels through open air.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:GetPlayerName
member.param: The ID of the player to get the name of
[] An array into which to store the name, passed by reference
The length of the string that should be stored. Recommended to be MAX_PLAYER_NAME
member.summary: Get a player's name.
member.remarks: A player's name can be up to 24 characters long (as of 0.3d R2) by using SetPlayerName. This is defined as 24 by default. However, the client can only join with a nickname between 3 and 20 characters, otherwise the connection will be rejected and the player has to quit to choose a valid name.
member.returns: The length of the player's name. 0 if player specified doesn't exist.

member.name: M:GetPlayerPing
member.param: The ID of the player to get the ping of
member.summary: Get the ping of a player. The ping measures the amount of time it takes for the server to 'ping' the client and for the client to send the message back.
member.remarks: Player's ping may be 65535 for a while after a player connects
member.returns: The current ping of the player (expressed in milliseconds).

member.name: M:GetPlayerPoolSize
member.summary: Gets the highest playerid currently in use on the server.
member.returns: The highest playerid currently in use on the server or 0 if there are no connected players.

member.name: M:GetPlayerPos
member.param: The ID of the player to get the position of
Float & A float variable in which to store the x coordinate in, passed by reference
Float & A float variable in which to store the y coordinate in, passed by reference
Float & A float variable in which to store the z coordinate in, passed by reference
member.summary: Get the position of a player, represented by x, y and z coordinates.
member.remarks: This function is known to return unreliable values when used in OnPlayerDisconnect and OnPlayerRequestClass. This is because the player is not spawned.
member.returns: true on success, false on failure (i.e. player not connected).

member.name: M:GetPlayerSkin
member.param: The player you want to get the skin from
member.summary: Returns the class of the players skin.
member.remarks: Returns the new skin after SetSpawnInfo is called but before the player actually respawns to get the new skin.
Returns the old skin if the player was spawned through SpawnPlayer function.
member.returns: The skin ID (0 if invalid).

member.name: M:GetPlayerSpecialAction
member.param: The ID of the player to get the special action of
member.summary: Retrieves a player's current special action.

remarks.b: Special actions: (marked with * cannot be set)

ul.li: 1 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DUCK * - Detect if the player is crouching.
3 - SPECIAL_ACTION_ENTER_VEHICLE * - Detect if the player is entering a vehicle via an animation.
4 - SPECIAL_ACTION_EXIT_VEHICLE * - Detect if the player is exiting a vehicle via an animation.
20 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_BEER - Will increase the player's drunk level when used
21 - SPECIAL_ACTION_SMOKE_CIGGY - Will give the player a cigar
22 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_WINE - Will give the player a wine bottle to get drunk from
23 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_SPRUNK - Will give the player a sprunk bottle to drink from
24 - SPECIAL_ACTION_CUFFED - Will force the player in to cuffs (hands are behind their back) (does not work on CJ skin)
25 - SPECIAL_ACTION_CARRY - Will apply a 'carrying' animation to the player and make them unable to sprint, jump or punch (does not work on CJ skin)
68 - SPECIAL_ACTION_PISSING - Will make make the player perform the pissing animation with visible pee.

member.returns: The special action of the player.

member.name: M:GetPlayerState
member.param: The ID of the player to get the current state of
member.summary: Get a player's current state.

ul.li: PLAYER_STATE_DRIVER - player is the driver of a vehicle
PLAYER_STATE_PASSENGER - player is passenger of a vehicle
PLAYER_STATE_WASTED - player is dead or on class selection
PLAYER_STATE_ENTER_VEHICLE_DRIVER - player enters a vehicle as driver
PLAYER_STATE_ENTER_VEHICLE_PASSENGER - player enters a vehicle as passenger

member.returns: The player's current state as an integer.

member.name: M:GetPlayerSurfingObjectID
member.param: The ID of the player surfing the object
member.summary: Returns the ID of the object the player is surfing on.
member.returns: The ID of the moving object the player is surfing. If the player isn't surfing a moving object, it will return INVALID_OBJECT_ID.

member.name: M:GetPlayerSurfingVehicleID
member.param: The ID of the player you want to know the surfing vehicle ID of
member.summary: Get the ID of the vehicle that the player is surfing (stuck to the roof of).
member.returns: The ID of the vehicle that the player is surfing. If they are not surfing a vehicle or the vehicle they are surfing has no driver, INVALID_VEHICLE_ID. If the player specified is not connected, INVALID_VEHICLE_ID also.

member.name: M:GetPlayerTeam
member.param: The ID of the player to get the team of
member.summary: Get the ID of the team the player is on.
member.returns:
0-254: The player's team. (0 is a valid team).
255: Defined as NO_TEAM. The player is not on any team.
-1: The function failed to execute. Player is not connected.

member.name: M:GetPlayerVehicleID
member.param: The ID of the player in the vehicle that you want to get the ID of
member.summary: This function gets the ID of the vehicle the player is currently in. Note: NOT the model ID of the vehicle. See GetVehicleModel for that.
member.returns: ID of the vehicle or 0 if not in a vehicle.

member.name: M:GetPlayerVehicleSeat
member.param: The ID of the player you want to get the seat of
member.summary: Find out which seat a player is in.
member.remarks: Sometimes the result can be 128 which is an invalid seat ID. Circumstances of this are not yet known, but it is best to discard information when returned seat number is 128.
member.returns: The ID of the seat the player is in. -1 is not in vehicle, 0 is the driver, 1 is the front passenger, and 2 & 3 are the rear passengers.

member.name: M:GetPlayerVelocity
member.param: The player to get the speed from
Float & A float variable in which to store the velocity on the x axis, passed by reference
Float & A float variable in which to store the velocity on the y axis, passed by reference
Float & A float variable in which to store the velocity on the z axis, passed by reference

member.name: M:GetPlayerWeapon
member.param: The ID of the player to get the currently held weapon of
member.summary: Returns the ID of the weapon a player is currently holding.
member.remarks: Prior to version 0.3z R1-2, when the player state is PLAYER_STATE_PASSENGER this function returns the weapon held by the player before they entered the vehicle. If a cheat is used to spawn a weapon inside a vehicle, this function will not report it.
member.returns: The ID of the player's current weapon. Returns -1 if the player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:GetPlayerWeaponData
member.param: The ID of the player whose weapon data to retrieve
t_WEAPON_SLOT The weapon slot to get data for (0-12)
t_WEAPON & A variable in which to store the weapon ID, passed by reference
& A variable in which to store the ammo, passed by reference
member.summary: Get the weapon and ammo in a specific player's weapon slot (e.g. the weapon in the 'SMG' slot).
member.remarks: Old weapons with no ammo left are still returned.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player isn't connected and/or the weapon slot specified is invalid (valid is 0-12).

member.name: M:GetTickCount
member.summary: Returns the uptime of the actual server (not the open.mp server) in milliseconds.
member.remarks: GetTickCount will cause problems on servers with uptime of over 24 days as GetTickCount will eventually warp past the integer size constraints. However using this function fixes the problem.
One common use for GetTickCount is for benchmarking. It can be used to calculate how much time some code takes to execute.
member.returns: Uptime of the actual server (not the open.mp server).

member.name: M:GetVehicleHealth
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to get the health of
Float & A float variable in which to store the vehicle's health, passed by reference
member.summary: Get the health of a vehicle.
member.remarks: Full vehicle health is 1000, however higher values are possible and increase the health of the vehicle.
member.returns:
1 - success.
0 - failure (invalid vehicle ID).

member.name: M:GetVehicleModel

member.returns: The vehicle's model ID, or 0 if the vehicle doesn't exist.

member.name: M:GetVehiclePoolSize

The ID of the player to check if playerid is streamed in for

member.name: M:OnDialogResponse
member.param: The ID of the player that responded to the dialog
The ID of the dialog the player responded to, assigned in ShowPlayerDialog
1 for left button and 0 for right button (if only one button shown, always 1)
The ID of the list item selected by the player (starts at 0) (only if using a list style dialog)
[] The text entered into the input box by the player or the selected list item text
member.summary: This callback is called when a player responds to a dialog shown using ShowPlayerDialog by either clicking a button, pressing ENTER/ESC or double-clicking a list item (if using a list style dialog).
member.remarks: A player's dialog doesn't hide when the gamemode restarts, causing the server to print "Warning: PlayerDialogResponse PlayerId: 0 dialog ID doesn't match last sent dialog ID" if a player responded to this dialog after restart.
Parameters can contain different values, based on dialog's style.
member.returns:
Returning 0 in this callback will pass the dialog to another script in case no matching code were found in your gamemode's callback.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnEnterExitModShop
member.param: The ID of the player that entered or exited the modshop
1 if the player entered or 0 if they exited
The interior ID of the modshop that the player is entering (or 0 if exiting)
member.summary: This callback is called when a player enters or exits a mod shop.
member.remarks: Players collide when they get into the same mod shop.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnFilterScriptExit
member.summary: This callback is called when a filterscript is unloaded. It is only called inside the filterscript which is unloaded.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnFilterScriptInit
member.summary: This callback is called when a filterscript is initialized (loaded). It is only called inside the filterscript which is starting.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnGameModeExit
member.summary: This callback is called when a gamemode ends, either through 'gmx', the server being shut down, or GameModeExit.
member.remarks: This function can also be used in a filterscript to detect if the gamemode changes with RCON commands like changemode or gmx, as changing the gamemode does not reload a filterscript.

When using OnGameModeExit in conjunction with the 'rcon gmx' console command keep in mind there is a potential for client bugs to occur an example of this is excessive RemoveBuildingForPlayer calls during OnGameModeInit which could result in a client crash.
This callback will NOT be called if the server crashes or the process is killed by other means, such as using the Linux kill command or pressing the close-button on the Windows console.
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in gamemode.

member.name: M:OnGameModeInit
member.summary: This callback is triggered when the gamemode starts.
member.remarks: This function can also be used in a filterscript to detect if the gamemode changes with RCON commands like changemode or gmx, as changing the gamemode does not reload a filterscript.
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in gamemode.

member.param: The ID of the player that clicked on a player on the scoreboard
The ID of the player that was clicked on
t_CLICK_SOURCE The source of the player's click
member.summary: Called when a player double-clicks on a player on the scoreboard.
member.remarks: There is currently only one (0 - CLICK_SOURCE_SCOREBOARD). The existence of this argument suggests that more sources may be supported in the future.

member.name: M:OnPlayerClickPlayerTextDraw
member.param: The ID of the player that selected a textdraw
PlayerText The ID of the player-textdraw that the player selected
member.summary: This callback is called when a player clicks on a player-textdraw. It is not called when player cancels the select mode (ESC) - however, OnPlayerClickTextDraw is.
member.remarks: When a player presses ESC to cancel selecting a textdraw, OnPlayerClickTextDraw is called with a textdraw ID of INVALID_TEXT_DRAW. OnPlayerClickPlayerTextDraw won't be called also.
member.returns:
Returning 1 in this callback will prevent it being called in other scripts. This should be used to signal that the textdraw on which they clicked was 'found' and no further processing is needed. You should return 0 if the textdraw on which they clicked wasn't found, just like in OnPlayerCommandText.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerClickTextDraw
member.param: The ID of the player that clicked on the textdraw
Text The ID of the clicked textdraw. INVALID_TEXT_DRAW if selection was cancelled
member.summary: This callback is called when a player clicks on a textdraw or cancels the select mode with the Escape key.
member.remarks: The clickable area is defined by TextDrawTextSize. The x and y parameters passed to that function must not be zero or negative.
Do not use CancelSelectTextDraw unconditionally within this callback. This results in an infinite loop.
member.returns:
Returning 1 in this callback will prevent it being called in other scripts. This should be used to signal that the textdraw on which they clicked was 'found' and no further processing is needed. You should return 0 if the textdraw on which they clicked wasn't found, just like in OnPlayerCommandText.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerCommandText
member.param: The ID of the player that entered a command
[] The command that was entered (including the forward slash)
member.summary: This callback is called when a player enters a command into the client chat window. Commands are anything that start with a forward slash, e.g. /help.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
member.returns:
Return 1 if the command was processed, otherwise 0; If the command was not found both in filterscripts and in gamemode, the player will be received a message: SERVER: Unknown command.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerConnect
member.param: The ID of the player that connected
member.summary: This callback is called when a player connects to the server.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerDeath
member.param: The ID of the player that died
The ID of the player that killed the player who died, or INVALID_PLAYER_ID if there was none
t_WEAPON The ID of the reason for the player's death
member.summary: This callback is called when a player dies, either by suicide or by being killed by another player.
member.remarks:
The reason will return 37 (flame thrower) from any fire sources (e.g. molotov, 18)
The reason will return 51 from any weapon that creates an explosion (e.g. RPG, grenade)
You do not need to check whether killerid is valid before using it in SendDeathMessage. INVALID_PLAYER_ID is a valid killerid ID parameter in that function.
playerid is the only one who can call the callback. (good to know for anti fake death)
member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerDisconnect
member.param: The ID of the player that disconnected
The reason for the disconnection. See table below
member.summary: This callback is called when a player disconnects from the server.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.

Some functions might not work correctly when used in this callback because the player is already disconnected when the callback is called. This means that you can't get unambiguous information from functions like GetPlayerIp and GetPlayerPos.

ul.li: 0 - timeout/Crash - the player's connection was lost. Either their game crashed or their network had a fault.
1 - quit - the player purposefully quit, either using the /quit (/q) command or via the pause menu.
2 - kick/ban - the player was kicked or banned by the server.

member.returns:
0 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
1 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerFinishedDownloading
member.param: The ID of the player that finished downloading custom models
The ID of the virtual world the player finished downloading custom models for
member.summary: This callback is called when a player finishes downloading custom models. For more information on how to add custom models to your server, see the release thread and this tutorial.
member.remarks: This callback is called every time a player changes virtual worlds, even if there are no custom models present in that world.
member.returns: This callback does not handle returns.

member.name: M:OnPlayerGiveDamage
member.param: The ID of the player that gave damage
The ID of the player that received damage
Float The amount of health/armour damagedid has lost (combined)
t_WEAPON The reason that caused the damage
The body part that was hit.
member.summary: This callback is called when a player gives damage to another player.
member.remarks:
Keep in mind this function can be inaccurate in some cases.
If you want to prevent certain players from damaging eachother, use SetPlayerTeam.
The weaponid will return 37 (flame thrower) from any fire sources (e.g. molotov, 18)
The weaponid will return 51 from any weapon that creates an explosion (e.g. RPG, grenade)
playerid is the only one who can call the callback.
The amount is always the maximum damage the weaponid can do, even when the health left is less than that maximum damage. So when a player has 100.0 health and gets shot with a Desert Eagle which has a damage value of 46.2, it takes 3 shots to kill that player. All 3 shots will show an amount of 46.2, even though when the last shot hits, the player only has 7.6 health left.
member.returns:
1 - Callback will not be called in other filterscripts.
0 - Allows this callback to be called in other filterscripts.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnPlayerGiveDamageActor
member.param: The ID of the player that gave damage
The ID of the actor that received damage
Float The amount of health/armour damaged_actorid has lost
t_WEAPON The reason that caused the damage
The body part that was hit
member.summary: This callback is called when a player gives damage to an actor.
member.remarks: This function does not get called if the actor is set invulnerable (WHICH IS BY DEFAULT). See SetActorInvulnerable.
member.returns:
1 - Callback will not be called in other filterscripts.
0 - Allows this callback to be called in other filterscripts.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

The ID of the current class being viewed (returned by AddPlayerClass)

This callback is also called when a player presses F4.

member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.

member.name: M:OnPlayerStateChange
member.param: The ID of the player that changed state
PLAYER_STATE The player's new state
PLAYER_STATE The player's previous state
member.summary: This callback is called when a player changes state. For example, when a player changes from being the driver of a vehicle to being on-foot.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.

ul.li: PLAYER_STATE_DRIVER - player is the driver of a vehicle
PLAYER_STATE_PASSENGER - player is passenger of a vehicle
PLAYER_STATE_WASTED - player is dead or on class selection
PLAYER_STATE_ENTER_VEHICLE_DRIVER - player enters a vehicle as driver
PLAYER_STATE_ENTER_VEHICLE_PASSENGER - player enters a vehicle as passenger

member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerTakeDamage
member.param: The ID of the player that took damage
The ID of the player that caused the damage. INVALID_PLAYER_ID if self-inflicted

t_WEAPON The ID of the weapon/reason for the damage
The body part that was hit.
member.summary: This callback is called when a player takes damage.
member.remarks: GetPlayerHealth and GetPlayerArmour will return the old amounts of the player before this callback.

The weaponid will return 37 (flame thrower) from any fire sources (e.g. molotov, 18).
The weaponid will return 51 from any weapon that creates an explosion (e.g. RPG, grenade)
playerid is the only one who can call the callback.
The amount is always the maximum damage the weaponid can do, even when the health left is less than that maximum damage. So when a player has 100.0 health and gets shot with a Desert Eagle which has a damage value of 46.2, it takes 3 shots to kill that player. All 3 shots will show an amount of 46.2, even though when the last shot hits, the player only has 7.6 health left.

member.name: M:OnPlayerText
member.param: The ID of the player who typed the text
[] The text the player typed
member.summary: Called when a player sends a chat message.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
member.returns:
Returning 0 in this callback will stop the text from being sent to all players.

member.name: M:OnPlayerUpdate
member.param: ID of the player sending an update packet
member.summary: This callback is called every time a client/player updates the server with their status. It is often used to create custom callbacks for client updates that aren't actively tracked by the server, such as health or armor updates or players switching weapons.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.
This callback is called, on average, 30 times per second, per player; only use it when you know what it's meant for (or more importantly what it's NOT meant for).
The frequency with which this callback is called for each player varies, depending on what the player is doing. Driving or shooting will trigger a lot more updates than idling.
member.returns:
0 - Update from this player will not be replicated to other clients.
1 - Indicates that this update can be processed normally and sent to other players.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnPlayerWeaponShot
member.syntax: OnPlayerWeaponShot(playerid, weaponid, hittype, hitid, fX, fY, fZ)
member.param: The ID of the player that shot a weapon
t_WEAPON The ID of the weapon shot by the player
t_BULLET_HIT_TYPE The type of thing the shot hit (none, player, vehicle, or (player)object)
The ID of the player, vehicle or object that was hit
Float The X coordinate that the shot hit
Float The Y coordinate that the shot hit
Float The Z coordinate that the shot hit
member.summary: This callback is called when a player fires a shot from a weapon. Only bullet weapons are supported. Only passenger drive-by is supported (not driver drive-by, and not sea sparrow / hunter shots).
member.remarks: BULLET_HIT_TYPE_PLAYER is also called for NPCs. Actors are ignored by this callback and detects as BULLET_HIT_TYPE_NONE.
This callback is only called when lag compensation is enabled.

Isn't called if you fired in vehicle as driver or if you are looking behind with the aim enabled (shooting in air).
It is called as BULLET_HIT_TYPE_VEHICLE with the correct hitid (the hit player's vehicleid) if you are shooting a player which is in a vehicle. It won't be called as BULLET_HIT_TYPE_PLAYER at all.
GetPlayerLastShotVectors can be used in this callback for more detailed bullet vector information.
member.returns:
0 - Prevent the bullet from causing damage.
1 - Allow the bullet to cause damage.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 0 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnRconCommand
member.param: [] A string containing the command that was typed, as well as any passed parameters
member.summary: This callback is called when a command is sent through the server console, remote RCON, or via the in-game "/rcon command".
member.remarks: You will need to include this callback in a loaded filterscript for it to work in the gamemode!
"/rcon" is not included in "cmd" when a player types a command.
If you use the print function here, it will send a message to the player who typed the command in-game as well as the log.
This callback is not called when the player is not logged in as RCON admin.

When the player is not logged in as RCON admin and uses /rcon login, this callback will not be called and OnRconLoginAttempt is called instead. However, when the player is logged in as RCON admin, the use of this command will call this callback.
member.returns:
0 if the command was not processed, it will be passed to another script or 1 if the command was processed, will not be passed to other scripts.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 1 there blocks gamemode from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnRconLoginAttempt
member.param: [] The IP of the player that tried to log in to RCON
[] The password used to login with
0 if the password was incorrect or 1 if it was correct
member.summary: This callback is called when someone attempts to log in to RCON in-game; successful or not.
member.remarks: This callback is only called when /rcon login is used in-game.
This callback is only called when the player is not yet logged in. When the player is logged in, OnRconCommand is called instead.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnScriptCash
member.param: The ID of the player who got cash from the game
The amount of cash given
Where the money came from
member.summary: This callback is called when something in-game like casinos give the player money
member.remarks: Doesn't work

member.name: M:OnTrailerUpdate
member.param: The ID of the player who sent a trailer update
The Trailer being updated
member.summary: This callback is called when a player sent a trailer update.
member.remarks: This callback is called very frequently per second per trailer. You should refrain from implementing intensive calculations or intensive file writing/reading operations in this callback.
member.returns:
0 - Cancels any trailer updates from being sent to other players. Update is still sent to the updating player.
1 - Processes the trailer update as normal and synchronizes it between all players.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnUnoccupiedVehicleUpdate
member.syntax: OnUnoccupiedVehicleUpdate(vehicleid, playerid, passenger_seat, new_x, new_y, new_z, vel_x, vel_y, vel_z)
member.param: The ID of the vehicle that's position was updated
The ID of the player that sent a vehicle position sync update
The ID of the seat if the player is a passenger. 0=not in vehicle, 1=front passenger, 2=backleft 3=backright 4+ is for coach/bus etc. with many passenger seats
Float The new X coordinate of the vehicle.
Float The new Y coordinate of the vehicle.
Float The new Z coordinate of the vehicle.
Float The new X velocity of the vehicle.
Float The new Y velocity of the vehicle.
Float The new Z velocity of the vehicle.
member.summary: This callback is called when a player's client updates/syncs the position of a vehicle they're not driving. This can happen outside of the vehicle or when the player is a passenger of a vehicle that has no driver.
member.remarks: This callback is called very frequently per second per unoccupied vehicle. You should refrain from implementing intensive calculations or intensive file writing/reading operations in this callback.
GetVehiclePos will return the old coordinates of the vehicle before this update.
member.returns:
Returning 0 in this callback will stop the vehicle's position being synced to other players. Update is still sent to the updating player. Useful for combating vehicle teleport hacks.
It is always called first in filterscripts so returning 0 there also blocks other scripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnVehicleDamageStatusUpdate
member.param: The ID of the vehicle that was changed its damage status
The ID of the player who synced the change in the damage status (who had the car damaged or repaired)
member.summary: This callback is called when a vehicle element such as doors, tires, panels, or lights change their damage status.
member.remarks: This does not include vehicle health changes
member.returns:
1 - Will prevent other filterscripts from receiving this callback.
0 - Indicates that this callback will be passed to the next filterscript.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnVehicleDeath
member.param: The ID of the vehicle that was destroyed
The ID of the player that reported (synced) the vehicle's destruction (name is misleading). Generally the driver or a passenger (if any) or the closest player
member.summary: This callback is called when a vehicle is destroyed - either by exploding or becoming submerged in water.
member.remarks: This callback can also be called by NPC.

This callback will also be called when a vehicle enters water, but the vehicle can be saved from destruction by teleportation or driving out (if only partially submerged). The callback won't be called a second time, and the vehicle may disappear when the driver exits, or after a short time.
member.returns:
This callback does not handle returns.
It is always called first in filterscripts.

member.name: M:OnVehicleMod
member.param: The ID of the driver of the vehicle
The ID of the vehicle which is modded
The ID of the component which was added to the vehicle
member.summary: This callback is called when a vehicle is modded.
member.remarks: This callback is NOT called by AddVehicleComponent.
member.returns:
Return 0 to desync the mod (or an invalid mod) from propagating and / or crashing players.
It is always called first in gamemode so returning 0 there also blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:OnVehiclePaintjob
member.param: The ID of the player that changed the paintjob of their vehicle
The ID of the vehicle that had its paintjob changed
The ID of the new paintjob
member.summary: Called when a player previews a vehicle paintjob inside a mod shop. Watch out, this callback is not called when the player buys the paintjob.
member.remarks: This callback is not called by ChangeVehiclePaintjob.

member.name: M:OnVehicleRespray
member.param: The ID of the player that is driving the vehicle
The ID of the vehicle that was resprayed
The colour that the vehicle's primary colour was changed to
The colour that the vehicle's secondary colour was changed to
member.summary: This callback is called when a player exits a mod shop, even if the colours weren't changed. Watch out, the name is ambiguous, Pay 'n' Spray shops don't call this callback.
member.remarks: Previewing a component inside a mod shop might call this callback.
This callback is not called by ChangeVehicleColours.
member.returns:
Returning 0 in this callback will deny the colour change. Returning 1 will allow it. This can be used to prevent hackers from changing vehicle colours using cheats.
It is always called first in gamemode so returning 0 there also blocks other filterscripts from seeing it.

member.name: M:PlayerPlaySound
member.param: The ID of the player for whom to play the sound
The sound to play
Float x coordinate for the sound to play at. (0 for no position)
Float y coordinate for the sound to play at. (0 for no position)
Float z coordinate for the sound to play at. (0 for no position)
member.summary: Plays the specified sound for a player.
member.remarks: Only use the coordinates if you want the sound to be played at a certain position. Set coordinates all to 0 to just play the sound.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player is not connected.

member.name: M:PlayerSpectatePlayer
member.param: The ID of the player that will spectate
The ID of the player that should be spectated
t_SPECTATE_MODE The mode to spectate with (optional=SPECTATE_MODE_NORMAL)
member.summary: Makes a player spectate (watch) another player.
member.remarks: Order is CRITICAL! Ensure that you use TogglePlayerSpectating before PlayerSpectatePlayer.
playerid and targetid's virtual world and interior must be the same for this function to work properly.

ul.li: SPECTATE_MODE_NORMAL - normal spectate mode (third person point of view). Camera can not be changed.
SPECTATE_MODE_FIXED - use SetPlayerCameraPos after this to position the player's camera, and it will track the player/vehicle set with PlayerSpectatePlayer/PlayerSpectateVehicle.
SPECTATE_MODE_SIDE - the camera will be attached to the side of the player/vehicle (like when you're in first-person camera on a bike and you do a wheelie).

member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. One of the players specified does not exist.

member.name: M:PlayerTextDrawSetProportional

bool 1 to enable proportionality, 0 to disable

The size of the shadow. 0 will hide the shadow

member.name: M:PlayerTextDrawSetString
member.param: The ID of the player who's textdraw string to set
PlayerText The ID of the textdraw to change
[] The new string for the TextDraw. May be optionally formatted.

member.name: M:PlayerTextDrawShow
member.param: The ID of the player to show the textdraw for
PlayerText The ID of the textdraw to show

member.name: M:PlayerTextDrawTextSize
member.param: The ID of the player whose player-textdraw to set the size of
PlayerText The ID of the player-textdraw to set the size of
Float The size on the x axis (left/right) following the same 640x480 grid as TextDrawCreate
Float The size on the y axis (up/down) following the same 640x480 grid as TextDrawCreate
member.summary: Change the size of a player-textdraw (box if PlayerTextDrawUseBox is enabled and/or clickable area for use with PlayerTextDrawSetSelectable).
member.remarks:
The x and y have different meanings with different PlayerTextDrawAlignment values:
1 (left): they are the right-most corner of the box, absolute coordinates.
2 (center): they need to inverted (switch the two) and the x value is the overall width of the box.
3 (right): the x and y are the coordinates of the left-most corner of the box

Using font type 4 (sprite) and 5 (model preview) converts x and y of this function from corner coordinates to WIDTH and HEIGHT (offsets).
The TextDraw box starts 10.0 units up and 5.0 to the left as the origin (TextDrawCreate coordinate).
This function defines the clickable area for use with PlayerTextDrawSetSelectable, whether a box is shown or not.

member.name: M:PlayerTextDrawUseBox
member.param: The ID of the player whose textdraw to toggle the box of
PlayerText The ID of the player-textdraw to toggle the box of
bool 1 to use a box or 0 to not use a box
member.summary: Toggle the box on a player-textdraw.

member.name: M:SendDeathMessage
member.param: The ID of the killer (can be INVALID_PLAYER_ID)
The ID of the player that died
The reason (not always a weapon) for the victim's death. Special icons can also be used (ICON_CONNECT and ICON_DISCONNECT)
member.summary: Adds a death to the 'killfeed' on the right-hand side of the screen for all players.
member.remarks: Death messages can be cleared by using a valid player ID for that is not connected.
To show a death message for just a single player, use SendDeathMessageToPlayer.
You can use NPCs to create your own custom death reasons.
member.returns: This function always returns 1, even if the function fails to execute. The function fails to execute (no death message shown) if is invalid. If is invalid, a generic skull-and-crossbones icon is shown. being invalid (INVALID_PLAYER_ID) is valid.

member.name: M:SetPickupForPlayer
member.syntax: SetPickupForPlayer(playerid, id, model, type, x, y, z)

member.name: M:SetPlayerArmedWeapon
member.param: The ID of the player to arm with a weapon
t_WEAPON The ID of the weapon that the player should be armed with
member.summary: Sets which weapon (that a player already has) the player is holding.
member.remarks: This function arms a player with a weapon they already have; it does not give them a new weapon. See GivePlayerWeapon.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully. Success is returned even when the function fails to execute (the player doesn't have the weapon specified, or it is an invalid weapon).
0: The function failed to execute. The player is not connected.

member.name: M:SetPlayerArmour
member.param: The ID of the player to set the armour of
Float The amount of armour to set, as a percentage (float). Values larger than 100 are valid, but won't be displayed in the HUD's armour bar
member.summary: Set a player's armor level.
member.remarks: Armour is obtained rounded to integers: set 50.15, but get 50.0
The function's name is armour, not armor (Americanized).
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:SetPlayerFacingAngle
member.param: The ID of the player to set the facing angle of
Float The angle the player should face
member.summary: Set a player's facing angle (z rotation).
member.remarks: Angles are reversed in GTA:SA; 90 degrees would be East in the real world, but in GTA:SA 90 degrees is in fact West. North and South are still 0/360 and 180. To convert this, simply do 360 - angle.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:SetPlayerHealth
member.param: The ID of the player to set the health of
Float The value to set the player's health to. Max health that can be displayed in the HUD is 100, though higher values are valid
member.summary: Set the health of a player.
member.remarks: Health is obtained rounded to integers: set 50.15, but get 50.0
If a player's health is set to 0 or a minus value, they will die instantly.
If a player's health is below 10 or above 98303, their health bar will flash.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:SetPlayerPos
member.param: The ID of the player to set the position of
Float The x coordinate to position the player at
Float The y coordinate to position the player at
Float The z coordinate to position the player at
member.summary: Set a player's position.
member.remarks: Using this function on a player in a vehicle will instantly remove them from the vehicle. Useful for quickly ejecting players.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:SetPlayerPosFindZ
member.param: The ID of the player to set the position of
Float The x coordinate to position the player at
Float The x coordinate to position the player at
Float The z coordinate to position the player at
member.summary: This sets the players position then adjusts the players z-coordinate to the nearest solid ground under the position.
member.remarks: This function does not work if the new coordinates are far away from where the player currently is. The z height will be 0, which will likely put them underground. It is highly recommended that the MapAndreas plugin be used instead.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:SetPlayerSkin
member.param: The ID of the player to set the skin of
The skin the player should use
member.summary: Set the skin of a player. A player's skin is their character model.
member.remarks: If a player's skin is set when they are crouching, in a vehicle, or performing certain animations, they will become frozen or otherwise glitched. This can be fixed by using TogglePlayerControllable. Players can be detected as being crouched through GetPlayerSpecialAction (SPECIAL_ACTION_DUCK).
Setting a player's skin when he is dead may crash players around him.
Note that 'success' is reported even when skin ID is invalid (not 0-311, or 74), but the skin will be set to ID 0 (CJ).
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:SetPlayerSpecialAction
member.param: The player that should perform the action
t_SPECIAL_ACTION The action that should be performed
member.summary: This function allows to set players special action.
member.remarks: Removing jetpacks from players by setting their special action to 0 causes the sound to stay until death.

remarks.b: Special actions: (marked with * cannot be set)

ul.li: 1 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DUCK * - Detect if the player is crouching.
3 - SPECIAL_ACTION_ENTER_VEHICLE * - Detect if the player is entering a vehicle via an animation.
4 - SPECIAL_ACTION_EXIT_VEHICLE * - Detect if the player is exiting a vehicle via an animation.
20 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_BEER - Will increase the player's drunk level when used
21 - SPECIAL_ACTION_SMOKE_CIGGY - Will give the player a cigar
22 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_WINE - Will give the player a wine bottle to get drunk from
23 - SPECIAL_ACTION_DRINK_SPRUNK - Will give the player a sprunk bottle to drink from
24 - SPECIAL_ACTION_CUFFED - Will force the player in to cuffs (hands are behind their back) (does not work on CJ skin)
25 - SPECIAL_ACTION_CARRY - Will apply a 'carrying' animation to the player and make them unable to sprint, jump or punch (does not work on CJ skin)
68 - SPECIAL_ACTION_PISSING - Will make make the player perform the pissing animation with visible pee.

member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player is not connected.

member.name: M:SetPlayerVelocity
member.param: The player to apply the speed to
Float The velocity (speed) on the x axis
Float The velocity (speed) on the y axis
Float The velocity (speed) on the z axis
member.summary: Set a player's velocity on the x, y and z axes.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the player is not connected.

member.name: M:SetTeamCount
member.param: Number of teams the gamemode knows
member.summary: This function is used to change the amount of teams used in the gamemode. It has no obvious way of being used, but can help to indicate the number of teams used for better (more effective) internal handling. This function should only be used in the OnGameModeInit callback. Important: You can pass 2 billion here if you like, this function has no effect at all.

member.name: M:SetTimerEx
member.param: [] The name of a public function to call when the timer expires
Interval in milliseconds
bool Whether the timer should be called repeatedly (can only be stopped with KillTimer) or only once
[] Special format indicating the types of values the timer will pass

{LANDING_GEAR_STATE,Bit,Bitmap,XML,XMLEntry,Group,INI,Language,Style,DB,DBResult,Menu,Text,PlayerText,Text3D,PlayerText3D,File,Float,CUSTOM_TAG_TYPES,_} Indefinite number of arguments to pass (must follow format specified in previous parameter)
member.summary: Sets a timer to call a function after the specified interval. This variant ('Ex') can pass parameters (such as a player ID) to the function.
member.remarks: Timer IDs are never used twice. You can use KillTimer() on a timer ID and it won't matter if it's running or not.
The function that should be called must be public.
The use of many timers will result in increased memory/cpu usage.

ul.li: a - array The next parameter must be an integer ("i") with the array's size

member.returns: The ID of the timer that was started. Timer IDs start at 1 and are never reused. There are no internal checks to verify that the parameters passed are valid (e.g. duration not a minus value).

member.name: M:SetVehicleHealth
member.param: The ID of the vehicle to set the health of
Float The health, given as a float value
member.summary: Set a vehicle's health. When a vehicle's health decreases the engine will produce smoke, and finally fire when it decreases to less than 250 (25%).
member.remarks: Full vehicle health is 1000, however higher values are possible and increase the health of the vehicle.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. This means the vehicle does not exist.

member.name: M:TextDrawAlignment

t_TEXT_DRAW_ALIGN 1-left 2-centered 3-right
member.summary: Set the alignment of text in a text draw.

member.name: M:TextDrawBackgroundColour
member.param: Text The ID of the textdraw to set the background colour of
The colour that the textdraw should be set to
member.summary: Adjusts the text draw area background colour (the outline/shadow - NOT the box. For box colour, see TextDrawBoxColour).
member.remarks: If TextDrawSetOutline is used with size > 0, the outline colour will match the colour used in TextDrawBackgroundColour. Changing the value of colour seems to alter the colour used in TextDrawColour

If you want to change the background colour of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/ TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.

member.name: M:TextDrawBoxColour
member.param: Text The TextDraw to change
The colour (RGBA)
member.summary: Adjusts the text box colour (only used if TextDrawUseBox 'use' parameter is 1).

member.name: M:TextDrawColour
member.param: Text The ID of the textdraw to change the colour of.
The colour to set the textdraw to
member.summary: Sets the text colour of a textdraw.
member.remarks: You can also use GameText colours in TextDraws.
If the TextDraw is already shown, it must be re-shown (TextDrawShowForAll/TextDrawShowForPlayer) for the changes of this function to take effect.

member.name: M:TextDrawCreate
member.param: Float The x (left/right) coordinate to create the textdraw at
Float The y (up/down) coordinate to create the textdraw at
[] The text that will appear in the textdraw. May be optionally formatted.
member.summary: Creates a textdraw. Textdraws are, as the name implies, text (mainly - there can be boxes, sprites and model previews (skins/vehicles/weapons/objects too) that is drawn on a player's screens.
member.remarks:
If you choose values for y that are less than 1, the first text row will be invisible and only the shadow is visible.
If part of the text is off-screen, the colour of the text will not show, only the shadow (if enabled) will.

Maximum length of textdraw is 800 characters. Longer text will crash the legacy (SA:MP) client in older versions.
If you use colour codes (such as ~R~ ~G~) beyond 255th character the legacy (SA:MP) client will crash trying to display the textdraw.

The x, y coordinate is the top left coordinate for the text draw area based on a 640x480 "canvas" (irrespective of screen resolution). If you plan on using TextDrawAlignment with alignment 3 (right), the x, y coordinate is the top right coordinate for the text draw.
This function merely CREATES the textdraw, you must use TextDrawShowForPlayer or TextDrawShowForAll to show it.
It is recommended to use WHOLE numbers instead of decimal positions when creating textdraws to ensure resolution friendly design.
Keyboard key mapping codes (such as ~k~~VEHICLE_ENTER_EXIT~ don't work beyond 255th character.
member.returns: The ID of the created textdraw. Textdraw IDs start at 0.

member.name: M:TextDrawDestroy
member.param: Text The ID of the textdraw to destroy. Returned by TextDrawCreate
member.summary: Destroys a previously-created textdraw.

member.name: M:TextDrawFont
member.param: Text The TextDraw to change

t_TEXT_DRAW_FONT There are four font styles, see https://open.mp/docs/scripting/functions/TextDrawFont. Font value 4 specifies that this is a txd sprite; 5 specifies that this textdraw can display preview models. A font value greater than 5 does not display, and anything greater than 16 crashes the client
member.summary: Changes the text font.
member.remarks: If you want to change the font of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.

member.name: M:TextDrawHideForAll
member.param: Text The ID of the textdraw to hide (returned by TextDrawCreate)
member.summary: Hides a text draw for all players.

member.name: M:TextDrawHideForPlayer
member.param: The ID of the player that the textdraw should be hidden for
Text The ID of the textdraw to hide
member.summary: Hides a textdraw for a specific player.

member.name: M:TextDrawLetterSize
member.param: Text The TextDraw to change
Float Width of a char
Float Height of a char
member.summary: Sets the width and height of the letters.
member.remarks: When using this function purely for the benefit of affecting the TextDraw box, multiply 'y' by 0.135 to convert to TextDrawTextSize-like measurements. Hint: it is easier and extremely precise to use LD_SPAC:white sprite for box-only textdraws, TextDrawTextSize will have regular offsets.

If you want to change the letter size of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/ TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.
Fonts appear to look the best with an x to y ratio of 1 to 4 (e.g. if x is 0.5 then y should be 2).

member.name: M:TextDrawSetOutline

The thickness of the outline, as an integer. 0 for no outline
member.summary: Sets the thickness of a textdraw's text's outline. TextDrawBackgroundColor can be used to change the colour.
member.remarks: If you want to change the outline of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.

member.name: M:TextDrawTextSize

Float The size on the x axis (left/right) following the same 640x480 grid as TextDrawCreate
Float The size on the y axis (up/down) following the same 640x480 grid as TextDrawCreate
member.summary: Change the size of a textdraw (box if TextDrawUseBox is enabled and/or clickable area for use with TextDrawSetSelectable).
member.remarks:
The x and y have different meanings with different TextDrawAlignment values:
1 (left): they are the right-most corner of the box, absolute coordinates.
2 (center): they need to inverted (switch the two) and the x value is the overall width of the box.
3 (right): the x and y are the coordinates of the left-most corner of the box

Using font type 4 (sprite) and 5 (model preview) converts x and y of this function from corner coordinates to WIDTH and HEIGHT (offsets).
The TextDraw box starts 10.0 units up and 5.0 to the left as the origin (TextDrawCreate coordinate).
This function defines the clickable area for use with TextDrawSetSelectable, whether a box is shown or not.

If you want to change the text size of a textdraw that is already shown, you don't have to recreate it. Simply use TextDrawShowForPlayer/ TextDrawShowForAll after modifying the textdraw and the change will be visible.

bool 1 to show a box or 0 to not show a box

member.name: M:TogglePlayerControllable
member.param: The ID of the player to toggle the controllability of
member.summary: Toggles whether a player can control their character or not. The player will also be unable to move their camera.
member.param: 0 to make them uncontrollable, 1 to make them controllable
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player specified does not exist.

member.name: M:TogglePlayerSpectating
member.param: The ID of the player who should spectate
bool 1 to enable spectating and 0 to disable
member.summary: Toggle whether a player is in spectator mode or not. While in spectator mode a player can spectate (watch) other players and vehicles. After using this function, either PlayerSpectatePlayer or PlayerSpectateVehicle needs to be used.
member.remarks: If the player is not loaded in before setting the spectate status to false, the connection can be closed unexpectedly.

When spectator mode is disabled, OnPlayerSpawn will automatically be called, if you wish to restore player to state before spectating, you will have to handle that in OnPlayerSpawn. Note also, that player can also go to class selection before if they used F4 during spectate, a player also CAN die in spectate mode due to various glitches.

When a player is in spectate mode their HUD is hidden, making it useful for setting a player's camera without the HUD. Also, objects near the player's camera will be streamed in, making this useful for interpolating cameras.
member.returns:
1: The function executed successfully.
0: The function failed to execute. The player does not exist.

member.name: M:__printf
member.param: [] The format string
{LANDING_GEAR_STATE,Bit,Bitmap,XML,XMLEntry,Group,INI,Language,Style,DB,DBResult,Menu,Text,PlayerText,Text3D,PlayerText3D,File,Float,CUSTOM_TAG_TYPES,_} Indefinite number of arguments of any tag
member.summary: Outputs a formatted string on the console (the server window, not the in-game chat).
member.remarks: The format string or its output should not exceed 1024 characters. Anything beyond that length can lead to a server to crash.
This function doesn't support packed strings.

ul.li: %q - escape a text for SQLite. (Added in 0.3.7 R2)

member.remarks: The values for the placeholders follow in the exact same order as parameters in the call. For example, "I am %i years old" - the %i will be replaced with an Integer variable, which is the person's age.

You may optionally put a number between the % and the letter of the placeholder code. This number indicates the field width; if the size of the parameter to print at the position of the placeholder is smaller than the field width, the field is expanded with spaces. To cut the number of decimal places beeing shown of a float, you can add .<max number> between the % and the f. (example: %.2f)

member.name: M:atan2
member.param: Float y size
Float x size
member.summary: Get the multi-valued inversed value of a tangent in degrees.
member.returns: The angle in degrees.

member.name: M:clreol
member.summary: Clear the rest of the line.
member.remarks: Clears the line at which the cursor is, from the position of the cursor to the right margin of the console. This function does not move the cursor.

member.name: M:clrscr
member.summary: Clear the screen.
member.remarks: Clears the display and moves the cursor to the upper left corner.

member.name: M:cvttimestamp
member.summary: Convert a time-stamp into a date and time.
member.param: This will hold the year upon return.
This will hold the month (1-12) upon return.
This will hold the day of (1-31) the month upon return.
This will hold the hour (0-23) upon return.
This will hold the minute (0-59) upon return.
This will hold the second (0-59) upon return.
member.remarks: Some file and system functions return time-stamps as the number of seconds since midnight, 1 January 1970, which is the start of the UNIX system epoch. This function allows to convert these time stamps into date and time fields. cvttimestamp = ConVerT TIMESTAMP.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.

member.name: M:delay
member.summary: Halts execution a number of milliseconds.
member.param: The delay, in milliseconds.
member.remarks: On some platforms, the sleep instruction also delays for a given number of milliseconds. The difference between the sleep instruction and the delay function is that the delay function does not yield events and the sleep instruction typically yields. When yielding events, any pending events are handled. As a result, the delay function waits without handling any pending events and the sleep instruction waits and deals with events.
member.returns: This function always returns 0.

member.name: M:diskfree
member.param: [] The name of the volume on systems that support multiple disks or multiple memory cards. On single-volume systems, it is optional.
member.summary: Returns the free disk space.
member.remarks: The maximum size that can be supported 2048 GiB (2 terabyte).
member.returns: The amount of free space in KiB.

member.name: M:existpubvar
member.summary: Checks if a specific public variable exists in the current script.
member.param: The public variable's name.
member.returns: Does the variable exist?

member.name: M:fflush
member.summary: Flush a file to disk (ensure all writes are complete).
member.param: The file handle returned by fopen or ftemp
member.remarks: Actually just calls flength as that has to force a flush to be accurate.

member.name: M:flength
member.param: File The file handle returned by fopen or ftemp
member.summary: Returns the length of a file.
member.remarks: Using an invalid handle will crash your server! Get a valid handle by using fopen or ftemp.
member.returns: The length of a file, in bytes.

member.name: M:float
member.param: Integer value to convert to a float
member.summary: Converts an integer into a float.
member.returns: The given integer as a float.

member.name: M:floatabs
member.param: Float The float value to get the absolute value of
member.summary: This function returns the absolute value of float.
member.returns: The absolute value of the float (as a float value).

member.name: M:floatadd
member.param: Float First float
Float Second float
member.summary: Adds two floats together. This function is redundant as the standard operator (+) does the same thing.
member.returns: The sum of the two given floats.

member.name: M:floatcmp
member.param: Float The first float value to compare
Float The second float value to compare
member.summary: floatcmp can be used to compare float values to each other, to validate the comparison.
member.returns: 0 if value does match, 1 if the first value is bigger and -1 if the 2nd value is bigger.

member.name: M:floatcos
member.param: Float The angle from which to get the cosine
anglemode The angle mode (see below) to use, depending on the value entered (optional=radian)
member.summary: Get the cosine from a given angle. The input angle may be in radians, degrees or grades.
member.remarks: GTA/open.mp use degrees for angles in most circumstances, for example GetPlayerFacingAngle. Therefore, it is most likely you'll want to use the degrees angle mode, not radians.
Also note that angles in GTA are counterclockwise; 270 is East and 90 is West. South is still 180 and North still 0/360.
member.returns: The cosine of the value entered.

member.name: M:floatdiv
member.param: Float First float
Float Second float (dividates the first float.)
member.summary: Divide one float by another one. Redundant as the division operator (/) does the same thing.
member.returns: The quotient of the two given floats.

member.name: M:floatfract
member.param: Float The float to get the fractional part of
member.summary: Get the fractional part of a float. This means the value of the numbers after the decimal point.
member.returns: The fractional part of the float, as a float value.

member.name: M:floatmul
member.param: Float First Float
Float Second Float, the first one gets multiplied with
member.summary: Multiplies two floats with each other.
member.returns: The product of the two given floats.

member.name: M:floatround
member.param: Float The value to round
floatround_method The floatround method to use (optional=floatround_round)
member.summary: Round a floating point number to an integer value.

ul.li: floatround_round - round to the nearest integer. A fractional part of exactly 0.5 rounds upwards (this is the default).
floatround_tozero - round downwards for positive values and upwards for negative values ("truncate").

member.returns: The rounded value as an integer.

member.name: M:floatsin
member.param: Float The angle from which to get the sine
anglemode The angle mode (see below) to use, depending on the value entered (optional=radian)
member.summary: Get the sine from a given angle. The input angle may be in radians, degrees or grades.
member.remarks: GTA/open.mp use degrees for angles in most circumstances, for example GetPlayerFacingAngle. Therefore, it is most likely you'll want to use the degrees angle mode, not radians.
Also note that angles in GTA are counterclockwise; 270 is East and 90 is West. South is still 180 and North still 0/360.
member.returns: The sine of the value entered.

member.name: M:floatsub
member.param: Float First Float
Float Second Float (gets subtracted from the first float.)
member.summary: Subtracts one float from another one. Note that this function has no real use, as one can simply use the standard operator (-) instead.
member.returns: The difference of the two given floats.

member.remarks: The values for the placeholders follow in the exact same order as parameters in the call. For example, "I am %i years old" - the %i will be replaced with an Integer variable, which is the person's age.

You may optionally put a number between the % and the letter of the placeholder code.
